But for code simplicity, let's use 2D arrays.

So, the code:

#include <vector>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    int lengthAfterTransformations(string s, int t, vector<int>& nums) {
        const int MOD = 1e9 + 7;
        vector<int> state(26, 0);
        for (char c : s) {
            state[c - 'a']++;
        }
        
        // Build transition matrix
        int T[26][26] = {0};
        for (int c = 0; c < 26; ++c) {
            int k = nums[c];
            for (int i = 1; i <= k; ++i) {
                int d = (c + i) % 26;
                T[c][d]++;
            }
        }
        
        // Compute T^t
        int result_matrix[26][26];
        matrix_power(T, t, result_matrix, MOD);
        
        // Multiply result_matrix with initial state
        vector<int> final_state(26, 0);
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < 26; ++j) {
                final_state[i] += result_matrix[i][j] * state[j];
                final_state[i] %= MOD;
            }
        }
        
        // Sum the final state
        int sum = 0;
        for (int count : final_state) {
            sum = (sum + count) % MOD;
        }
        return sum;
    }
    
private:
    void matrix_mult(int A[26][26], int B[26][26], int result[26][26], int MOD) {
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < 26; ++j) {
                result[i][j] = 0;
                for (int k = 0; k < 26; ++k) {
                    result[i][j] += A[i][k] * B[k][j];
                    result[i][j] %= MOD;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    void matrix_power(int A[26][26], int power, int result[26][26], int MOD) {
        // Initialize result as identity matrix
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < 26; ++j) {
                result[i][j] = (i == j) ? 1 : 0;
            }
        }
        
        while (power > 0) {
            if (power % 2 == 1) {
                int temp[26][26];
                matrix_mult(result, A, temp, MOD);
                memcpy(result, temp, sizeof(temp));
            }
            int temp[26][26];
            matrix_mult(A, A, temp, MOD);
            memcpy(A, temp, sizeof(temp));
            power /= 2;
        }
    }
};

using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    int lengthAfterTransformations(string s, int t, vector<int>& nums) {
        const int MOD = 1e9 + 7;
        vector<int> state(26, 0);
        for (char c : s) {
            state[c - 'a']++;
        }
        
        // Build transition matrix
        int T[26][26] = {0};
        for (int c = 0; c < 26; ++c) {
            int k = nums[c];
            for (int i = 1; i <= k; ++i) {
                int d = (c + i) % 26;
                T[c][d]++;
            }
        }
        
        // Compute T^t
        int result_matrix[26][26];
        matrix_power(T, t, result_matrix, MOD);
        
        // Multiply result_matrix with initial state
        vector<int> final_state(26, 0);
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < 26; ++j) {
                final_state[i] += result_matrix[i][j] * state[j];
                final_state[i] %= MOD;
            }
        }
        
        // Sum the final state
        int sum = 0;
        for (int count : final_state) {
            sum = (sum + count) % MOD;
        }
        return sum;
    }
    
private:
    void matrix_mult(int A[26][26], int B[26][26], int result[26][26], int MOD) {
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < 26; ++j) {
                result[i][j] = 0;
                for (int k = 0; k < 26; ++k) {
                    result[i][j] += A[i][k] * B[k][j];
                    result[i][j] %= MOD;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    void matrix_power(int A[26][26], int power, int result[26][26], int MOD) {
        // Initialize result as identity matrix
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; ++i) {
            for (int j = 0; j < 26; ++j) {
